title: AI vs Generative AI vs Agentic AI
summary: Distinguish classical AI (rules + ML), Generative AI (content creation via LLMs/diffusion), and Agentic AI (autonomous multi-step reasoning with tools) — and when each architecture applies.

title: AI Product Maturity: MVP → RAG → MCP
summary: Trace the three-stage evolution from a basic LLM prompt (MVP) to retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to fully agentic systems using Model Context Protocol (MCP) as the tool-use standard.

title: Fine-Tuning vs RAG vs Prompting
summary: Choose the right strategy when a base LLM falls short — understand when to fine-tune on your data, when RAG is a cheaper alternative, when few-shot prompting is enough, and when LoRA/QLoRA makes fine-tuning practical.

title: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning
summary: Compare all three ML paradigms by data requirements, feedback mechanism, and real-world use cases.

title: Bias vs Variance Tradeoff
summary: Understand the fundamental tension between a model that assumes too much (high bias) and one that memorizes noise (high variance), and how regularization, ensemble methods, and data size each move the needle.

title: Overfitting vs Underfitting
summary: Diagnose bias-variance behavior and use validation signals to pick model complexity safely.

title: Accuracy, Precision, Recall & F1 Score
summary: Choose the right classification metric for imbalanced datasets, fraud detection, and medical diagnosis — when accuracy lies and F1 tells the truth.

title: Decision Tree, Random Forest & XGBoost
summary: From a single decision tree to bagged forests and boosted ensembles — understand how each builds on the last, when tree-based models beat neural networks, and why XGBoost wins most tabular data competitions.

title: Linear vs Logistic Regression
summary: Understand linear regression for continuous output prediction and logistic regression for binary classification — the baseline algorithms every ML engineer must know before reaching for a more complex model.

title: L1 vs L2 Regression
summary: Compare sparse feature selection and coefficient shrinkage strategies under real model constraints.

title: Embeddings & Vector Search
summary: Convert text, images, and code into dense vector representations and retrieve semantically similar results at scale using ANN algorithms — the core primitive behind RAG and semantic search.

title: Transformer & Attention Mechanism
summary: Understand how self-attention, multi-head attention, and positional encoding enable transformers to model long-range dependencies — the architecture powering every modern LLM.

title: Activation Functions
summary: Compare Softmax, ReLU, Sigmoid, and Tanh — understand what each activation does to a neuron's output, where each belongs in a network, and why the choice matters for training stability and model performance.

title: Design Patterns for AI
summary: Catalog reusable AI system patterns — RAG pipeline, agent loop, chain-of-thought, few-shot prompting, tool use, and output guardrails — with trade-offs and when-to-use guidance.

title: n8n vs LangGraph: Automation vs Orchestration
summary: Deterministic trigger-action automation (n8n) vs dynamic LLM-driven orchestration (LangGraph) — understand who controls the flow, when to use each, and how they can work together.

title: RAG vs LangChain vs LangGraph
summary: Compare raw embedding retrieval, full RAG pipelines, LangChain chain orchestration, and LangGraph stateful agent workflows — pick the right abstraction level for production LLM systems.

title: Gradient Descent vs SGD vs ALS
summary: Choose the right optimizer path based on data size, convergence profile, and matrix-factorization needs.

title: Business Objectives
summary: Align visual search design with conversion growth, discovery quality, and catalog utilization goals.

title: Key Interview Questions
summary: Prepare architecture and scalability responses for high-volume production ML system interviews.

title: System Components
summary: Break down ingestion, embedding, vector search, and serving layers in a practical production stack.

title: Success Metrics
summary: Track outcome KPIs and latency-quality metrics to evaluate both business and system health.

title: Visual Search - ML
summary: Review architecture references and system diagrams for an end-to-end visual similarity platform.

title: Google Street Blurring - ML
summary: Explore a privacy-sensitive image processing pipeline with large-scale geospatial ingestion patterns.

title: Google Cloud AI Learning Path
summary: Structured walkthrough of the 4-course GCP AI fundamentals path — Intro to GenAI, Intro to LLMs, Responsible AI, and Prompt Design in Vertex AI — with key takeaways and certification checkpoints.

title: Performance Matrices
summary: Load time, TTFB, DOM readiness, render-blocking resources, and round-trip time baselines for production web systems.

title: System Reliability
summary: CAP theorem trade-offs, consistent hashing, load balancing, rate limiting, and observability stack selection.

title: Key System Attributes
summary: Latency, throughput, and scalability definitions used as primary evaluation axes in production system reviews.

title: API Performance
summary: Stateless vs stateful API design, connection pooling, caching strategies, compression, and async workload handling.

title: Design Patterns
summary: MVC, MVP, 3-Tier, Singleton, Factory, Observer, Strategy — context on when each resolves coupling and maintainability issues.

title: Monolithic vs Microservices
summary: Trade-off analysis for monolithic, modular-monolithic, and microservices deployment topologies.

title: Front-end Performance
summary: Minification, bundling, lazy loading, image optimization, and critical rendering path techniques for production SPA delivery.

title: GraphQL vs REST
summary: Practical trade-offs between GraphQL flexible querying and REST simplicity, caching, and tooling support.

title: Protocols
summary: HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, SMTP, and gRPC usage contexts and trade-offs in distributed system communication layers.

title: Database Performance
summary: Indexing strategies, query optimization, connection pooling, sharding, and replication for production data layers.

title: Database Types
summary: Relational, NoSQL, in-memory, graph, and time-series databases compared with sharding and partitioning strategies.

title: Kafka vs Redis
summary: Event streaming platform vs in-memory store: when to use distributed message queues vs caching layers.

title: DevOps & Deployment
summary: CI/CD, IaC, monitoring, containerization, orchestration, and region-based traffic routing for cloud-native deployments.

title: Networking
summary: Load balancing, firewalls, VPNs, DNS, CDN, and network monitoring essentials for distributed systems.

title: Communication Models
summary: Synchronous vs asynchronous communication patterns and their impact on system coupling and fault tolerance.
